chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-2350 of 2024 (Ali Akbar and others V. Province of Sindh and others) Date Order with signature of Judge(s) Before: Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi For hearing of Main Case. Date of hearing and Order 12.02.2026 Mr. Sultan Ahmed, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Masroor Ahmed, Advocate for Respondent/KW&SC. ********** Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. – Petitioners Ali Akbar, Haji Karam Khan, Muhammad Juman Jokhio and Azeem Khan have filed this Constitution Petition under Article 199 of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan 1973 seeking following relief: 1. Declare that Petitioners being retired employees are legally entitled for recovery of retirement benefits, group insurance, gratuity etc. from the respondents and respondents are bound to act under framework provided in constitution of Pakistan and to obey the law being the statutory body incorporated under the provincial government. 2. To direct the Respondents to release all the retirement benefits group insurance, gratuity forthwith without committing any further delay as the respondents are bound by law to act in accordance with statutes and recovery of dues is the fundamental right of the Petitioners and respondents are bound under the principle policy to do so. 3. Any other relief this Honorable Court may deem appropriate and proper in the circumstances of the case. 4. Grant costs of the Petition. 2. Pursuant to orders of this Court, the Respondent was directed to clear all outstanding dues payable to the petitioners. In compliance of the said orders, the Respondent has duly discharged all liabilities and paid the entire outstanding dues to the petitioners.

Pursuant to orders of this Court, the Respondent was directed to clear all outstanding dues payable to the petitioners. In compliance of the said orders, the Respondent has duly discharged all liabilities and paid the entire outstanding dues to the petitioners. The compliance report is reproduced as under:- 1 Haji Karam Khan Petitioner No.2 All Dues Cleared 2 Muhammad Juman Jokhio Petitioner No.3 All Dues Cleared 3 Azeem Khan Petitioner No.4 All Dues Cleared 4 Ali Akbar Petitioner No.1 All amount cleared except LPR amount Rs.395,612/- is outstanding for payment, however this amount shall be cleared/ paid in the first week of March, 2026. 2 3. So far as LPR amount of Petitioner No.1 Ali Akbar is concerned, learned counsel for Respondent undertakes that the same shall be cleared in the first week of March, 2026. 4. The petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Ayaz Gul
